title: ABI, Shared Libraries, and Foreign-Function Memory · Memory Trace: Buffer Length Allocator Family
summary: A C library shares opaque handles and buffers with Rust, Python, and WebAssembly consumers that use different allocators and unwind rules. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem buffer length allocator family increment in versioned cross-language ownership ABI. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Pass buffers with lengths, ownership direction, allocator family, and release callback across FFI.
Expose stable opaque handles with versioned constructors, operations, observers, and same-library destruction.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
